name: Valor Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering personal and business checking, savings, CDs, and lending focused on local impact and nonprofit giving.
description_long:
Valor Bank was established in 2014 when local bankers and business leaders purchased First State Bank with the mission to build a bank for the community, not just in it. The bank rebranded to Valor Bank in 2015 under the leadership of President & CEO Josh Litsch, with a founding philosophy centered on faithfulness to commitments, valuing people, and courage to follow God's will. The organization operates as a locally-owned institution committed to community reinvestment and social responsibility.

Valor Bank offers FDIC-insured deposit products including free personal checking accounts (no monthly fees, no minimum balance), interest-bearing Valor Checking, business checking ($10/month with unlimited ACHs), and Certificate of Deposit (CD) products (12-month CDs at 3.65% APY with $1,000 minimum). The bank provides real estate loans, personal lending, and business lending services. Banking access occurs through 24/7 online and mobile platforms, with services including check mobile deposit, fund transfers, bill pay, wire transfers (free incoming, $10 outgoing), and ATM access.

Valor Bank distinguishes itself through its commitment to community impact: the organization donates at least 10% of profits to local nonprofits addressing community needs. The bank emphasizes a "Follow the Dollar" transparency model showing how customer deposits fund local loans and community giving. As a locally-owned institution rather than a national chain, Valor positions itself on relationship-based banking with dedicated customer success focus. The company's values-driven approach—including the explicit integration of faith-based principles in its mission—sets it apart from mainstream banking competitors.

Valor Bank is legitimately FDIC-insured and regulated (NMLS #746723), making it a genuine banking option rather than a fintech or alternative lender. However, as a community bank, it likely has limited physical locations and geographic service area. The website does not display competitive interest rate comparisons, loan terms, or approval requirements, making it difficult to assess competitiveness against larger banks. There is no information about business hours, account features beyond basic descriptions, or specific lending criteria. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Free personal checking accounts (no monthly fees, no minimum balance)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Free personal checking with no monthly fees and no minimum balance requirements
Interest-bearing checking account (Valor Checking) with no fees
Donates at least 10% of profits to local nonprofits, creating measurable community impact
24/7 online and mobile banking access with mobile check deposit
Business checking includes unlimited ACHs ($10/month) with transparent wire fee structure
Competitive 12-month CD rate at 3.65% APY (as of March 2026)
cons: Limited to local community service area as a regional bank; likely not available nationwide
No information provided about competitive rates across product categories or comparison data
Website lacks details on account approval process, credit requirements, or lending qualification criteria
No mention of overdraft protection, account features beyond basic descriptions, or ancillary services
Faith-based institutional philosophy may not align with all consumer preferences
best_for: Community-minded depositors who prioritize local nonprofit giving and social impact banking
Small business owners in the service area seeking relationship-based lending and business banking
Individuals seeking FDIC-insured checking with no fees and transparent, values-aligned banking
Real estate investors and borrowers seeking local, community-focused lending partnerships
diagnosis: Valor Bank is best for community-minded depositors and local small business owners in its service area who prioritize values-aligned banking with measurable nonprofit giving. The main caveat is that as a regional community bank, it operates in limited geographic locations and does not provide competitive rate or lending requirement information on its public website, requiring direct contact to assess product competitiveness.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
